evals.name: discount-fuzzy-to-list
evals.grounding: evalsPlan §evals.json: one behavioral eval — fuzzy 'add gold-tier discounts to checkout' → JSON acceptance test-list + cut. Checks: C0 scopeSafe regex on every proposedTestName (deterministic); C1 falsifiable+deterministic on every NOW item (deterministic); C2 cutReason present on every non-now item (deterministic); C3 Canon-TDD mechanism-token deny-list — regex catches OBVIOUS tokens (fn calls, import, class, src/ paths) deterministically; C4 layer enum legal + L3 items justify journey (deterministic); C5 two-projects-plus-cut shape (≥2 NOW families + ≥1 PUNT/LATER) (deterministic); C6 atomicity (token count == item count) (deterministic); C7 Canon-TDD natural-language smuggling fixtures — adversarial criteria that hide a mechanism in prose ('by calling the SendGrid client', 'iterate the array and push to results', 'use a hashmap to dedupe'); asserts the deny-list catches the obvious (deterministic FLOOR) and honestly marks subtler prose as MODEL-JUDGE REQUIRED (regex is a floor not a ceiling — no overclaim).
evals.prompt:
다음 fuzzy 요청을 spec-first 5-step de-risk 프로토콜로 분석하고, 결과를 JSON 배열로만 출력해줘 (마크다운 없이 raw JSON).

요청: "골드 티어 고객 할인을 체크아웃에 추가해줘"

JSON 배열의 각 항목은 다음 필드를 반드시 포함해야 해:
- id: string (proposedTestName과 동일, /^[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+$/ 패턴)
- proposedTestName: string (id와 동일)
- criterion: string (관찰 가능한 결과 주장, 메커니즘/함수/파일명 언급 금지)
- layer: string (l0, l1, l2, l3 중 하나)
- falsifiable: boolean
- deterministic: boolean
- cut: string (now, punt, later 중 하나)
- cutReason: string (cut이 now가 아닐 때 필수)

그리고 JSON 배열 뒤에 줄바꿈 후 CUT_RATIONALE: 로 시작하는 한 줄을 추가해줘.

checks.grounding: evalsPlan C0: every item's proposedTestName matches /^[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+$/ — the slice {scope} token and recurrence handle. recursive-slice.js:611 scopeSafe guard. Any item failing this breaks the filterCommand replace and the --list-scopes round-trip.
checks.cmd: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const raw=fs.readFileSync(process.env.EVAL_OUTPUT,'utf8'); const jsonText=raw.match(/\\[.*?\\]/s)?.[0]; if(!jsonText) throw new Error('no JSON array found'); const items=JSON.parse(jsonText); const re=/^[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+$/; const bad=items.filter(i=>!re.test(i.proposedTestName||'')); if(bad.length) throw new Error('proposedTestName fails scopeSafe regex: '+bad.map(i=>i.proposedTestName).join(', ')); console.log('C0 OK: all',items.length,'proposedTestNames pass /^[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+$/');"
checks.desc: Every item's proposedTestName matches /^[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+$/ (the slice {scope} and recurrence handle)

checks.grounding: evalsPlan C1: every NOW item has falsifiable:true && deterministic:true — the two mandatory gates of the spec-first discipline. A NOW item that isn't falsifiable is vacuous; one that isn't deterministic cannot be a slice scope token.
checks.cmd: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const raw=fs.readFileSync(process.env.EVAL_OUTPUT,'utf8'); const jsonText=raw.match(/\\[.*?\\]/s)?.[0]; if(!jsonText) throw new Error('no JSON array found'); const items=JSON.parse(jsonText); const nowItems=items.filter(i=>i.cut==='now'); const bad=nowItems.filter(i=>i.falsifiable!==true||i.deterministic!==true); if(bad.length) throw new Error('NOW items missing falsifiable/deterministic: '+bad.map(i=>i.id).join(', ')); if(nowItems.length===0) throw new Error('no NOW items found'); console.log('C1 OK:',nowItems.length,'NOW items all falsifiable+deterministic');"
checks.desc: Every NOW item has falsifiable:true and deterministic:true (the two spec-first gates)

checks.grounding: evalsPlan C2: every item with cut!='now' carries a non-empty cutReason. Silent drops are forbidden — PUNT/LATER items are named with a reason (scope-floor items cannot be silently punted).
checks.cmd: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const raw=fs.readFileSync(process.env.EVAL_OUTPUT,'utf8'); const jsonText=raw.match(/\\[.*?\\]/s)?.[0]; if(!jsonText) throw new Error('no JSON array found'); const items=JSON.parse(jsonText); const deferred=items.filter(i=>i.cut&&i.cut!=='now'); const bad=deferred.filter(i=>!i.cutReason||i.cutReason.trim()===''); if(bad.length) throw new Error('non-now items missing cutReason: '+bad.map(i=>i.id).join(', ')); console.log('C2 OK:',deferred.length,'deferred items all carry cutReason');"
checks.desc: Every non-now item carries a non-empty cutReason (no silent drops)

checks.grounding: evalsPlan C3: Canon-TDD audit — no item's criterion or other fields name an implementation mechanism. This check is DETERMINISTIC but PARTIAL: the deny-list regex catches OBVIOUS mechanism tokens — function calls (.send(, .call(), import statements, class/function/def keywords, src/ paths, require( — plus a few unambiguous natural-language HOW phrasings ('by calling ', 'use a/an <datastructure>', 'iterate the/over ', 'push to ', 'loop through'). It does NOT and CANNOT catch all prose smuggling; subtler natural-language mechanism leakage is the job of the MODEL-JUDGED C7 check. Honest framing: a green C3 means no obvious tokens, not 'provably WHAT-only'. Items should describe observable outcomes (WHAT), not implementation steps (HOW).
checks.cmd: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const raw=fs.readFileSync(process.env.EVAL_OUTPUT,'utf8'); const jsonText=raw.match(/\\[.*?\\]/s)?.[0]; if(!jsonText) throw new Error('no JSON array found'); const items=JSON.parse(jsonText); const denyList=/\\.(?:send|call)\\(|\\bimport\\s|\\bclass\\s|\\bfunction\\s|src\/|require\\(|def\\s+\\w+\\(|void\\s+\\w+|\\bby calling\\b|\\biterate (?:the|over)\\b|\\bpush to\\b|\\bloop through\\b|\\buse a(?:n)? (?:hashmap|hash map|dictionary|array|list|set|stack|queue|map)\\b/i; const bad=[]; items.forEach(i=>{ const fields=[i.criterion,i.given,i.when,i.then,i.assertion].filter(Boolean).join(' '); if(denyList.test(fields)) bad.push(i.id+': '+fields.slice(0,80)); }); if(bad.length) throw new Error('mechanism tokens found (Canon-TDD violation):\n'+bad.join('\n')); console.log('C3 OK: no OBVIOUS mechanism tokens in',items.length,'items (subtle prose smuggling -> C7 model-judged)');"
checks.desc: No item's criterion/fields contain an OBVIOUS mechanism token (fn call, import, class, src/ path, or unambiguous HOW phrasing like 'by calling'/'iterate the'/'use a hashmap') — Canon TDD WHAT-not-HOW, deterministic-but-partial (subtle prose -> C7)

checks.grounding: evalsPlan C4: every item's layer is in {l0,l1,l2,l3}; L3 items must justify a journey (not manufactured without an existing checkout/E2E journey context). The discount scenario has a checkout journey, so L3 is allowed but the criterion must reference an end-to-end user flow.
checks.cmd: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const raw=fs.readFileSync(process.env.EVAL_OUTPUT,'utf8'); const jsonText=raw.match(/\\[.*?\\]/s)?.[0]; if(!jsonText) throw new Error('no JSON array found'); const items=JSON.parse(jsonText); const validLayers=new Set(['l0','l1','l2','l3']); const badLayer=items.filter(i=>!validLayers.has(i.layer)); if(badLayer.length) throw new Error('illegal layer value: '+badLayer.map(i=>i.id+'='+i.layer).join(', ')); const l3items=items.filter(i=>i.layer==='l3'); const journeyKeywords=/login|cart|checkout|flow|journey|buy|purchase|receipt|sign.?up|onboard|end.?to.?end|e2e|user.*step|step.*user/i; const unjustified=l3items.filter(i=>{ const text=[i.criterion,i.given,i.when,i.then,i.assertion].filter(Boolean).join(' '); return !journeyKeywords.test(text); }); if(unjustified.length) throw new Error('L3 items lack journey justification: '+unjustified.map(i=>i.id).join(', ')); console.log('C4 OK: layers legal; L3 items:',l3items.length,'(all journey-justified)');"
checks.desc: Every layer is in {l0,l1,l2,l3}; L3 items reference a journey flow (not manufactured)

checks.grounding: evalsPlan C5: the list yields ≥2 distinct NOW scope-token families (e.g. discount_* pure-logic family + checkout_* integration family) + ≥1 PUNT or LATER item. This is the 'two small projects + one thing to cut' output gate — if the list cannot decompose into this shape, ambiguity was not sufficiently reduced.
checks.cmd: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const raw=fs.readFileSync(process.env.EVAL_OUTPUT,'utf8'); const jsonText=raw.match(/\\[.*?\\]/s)?.[0]; if(!jsonText) throw new Error('no JSON array found'); const items=JSON.parse(jsonText); const nowItems=items.filter(i=>i.cut==='now'); const deferredItems=items.filter(i=>i.cut&&i.cut!=='now'); if(deferredItems.length<1) throw new Error('no PUNT/LATER items — two-projects-plus-cut shape requires ≥1 deferred item'); const families=new Set(nowItems.map(i=>{ const tok=i.id||i.proposedTestName||''; const prefix=tok.split(/[_.-]/)[0]; return prefix; })); if(families.size<2) throw new Error('only '+families.size+' NOW token family (need ≥2 for two-projects shape): '+[...families].join(', ')); console.log('C5 OK:',families.size,'NOW families ('+[...families].join(',')+');',deferredItems.length,'PUNT/LATER');"
checks.desc: List yields ≥2 distinct NOW scope-token families + ≥1 PUNT/LATER (the two-small-projects-plus-cut output gate)

checks.grounding: evalsPlan C6: atomicity — token count == item count == distinct behavior claims. No bundled items (one item must not assert multiple behaviors). Verified by checking that all proposedTestNames are unique across the list (a bundled item would reuse or duplicate a token).
checks.cmd: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const raw=fs.readFileSync(process.env.EVAL_OUTPUT,'utf8'); const jsonText=raw.match(/\\[.*?\\]/s)?.[0]; if(!jsonText) throw new Error('no JSON array found'); const items=JSON.parse(jsonText); if(items.length===0) throw new Error('empty item list'); const tokens=items.map(i=>i.proposedTestName||i.id); const unique=new Set(tokens); if(unique.size!==tokens.length) { const dupes=tokens.filter((t,i)=>tokens.indexOf(t)!==i); throw new Error('duplicate proposedTestNames (bundled items violate atomicity): '+[...new Set(dupes)].join(', ')); } console.log('C6 OK:',items.length,'items, all tokens unique (1 item = 1 behavior = 1 scope token)');"
checks.desc: All proposedTestNames are unique — token count == item count, no bundled multi-behavior items (atomicity)

checks.grounding: evalsPlan C7: the HONEST companion to C3, run as a self-contained fixture check (no EVAL_OUTPUT). It pins a corpus of ADVERSARIAL natural-language smuggling cases — criteria that hide an implementation mechanism in prose that reads like an outcome — and asserts the C3 deny-list's behaviour on them, drawing an explicit line between what the regex CAN catch deterministically and what it CANNOT (and therefore must be model-judged). HONEST FRAMING (the whole point of this check): the regex is a floor, not a ceiling. Category A (regex-catchable) — phrasings with an unambiguous HOW token the C3 deny-list flags: 'by calling the SendGrid client', 'iterate the array and push to results', 'use a hashmap to dedupe order ids'. Category B (regex-EVADES, model-judged) — mechanism smuggled so smoothly the regex misses it: 'the confirmation is dispatched through our transactional provider', 'duplicates are collapsed by tracking seen ids in a side structure', 'the rows are walked one by one until a match surfaces'. The check ASSERTS the regex flags every Category-A fixture (deterministic floor holds) AND ASSERTS the regex does NOT flag Category-B (proving the gap is real), then PRINTS the Category-B fixtures as 'MODEL-JUDGE REQUIRED' so the harness's human/model reviewer adjudicates them. It does NOT claim to decide Category B deterministically — that would overclaim. The correct WHAT-only rewrites are also pinned: 'confirmation email delivered to verified address within 60s'; 'duplicate order ids collapse to exactly one persisted record'; 'lookup returns the matching record or none'.
checks.cmd: node -e "const denyList=/\\.(?:send|call)\\(|\\bimport\\s|\\bclass\\s|\\bfunction\\s|src\/|require\\(|def\\s+\\w+\\(|void\\s+\\w+|\\bby calling\\b|\\biterate (?:the|over)\\b|\\bpush to\\b|\\bloop through\\b|\\buse a(?:n)? (?:hashmap|hash map|dictionary|array|list|set|stack|queue|map)\\b/i; const catA=['the confirmation is sent by calling the SendGrid client','iterate the array and push matches to results','use a hashmap to dedupe order ids']; const catB=['the confirmation is dispatched through our transactional provider','duplicates are collapsed by tracking seen ids in a side structure','the rows are walked one by one until a match surfaces']; const missedA=catA.filter(s=>!denyList.test(s)); if(missedA.length) throw new Error('C3 deny-list FLOOR broken — failed to flag obvious smuggling: '+JSON.stringify(missedA)); const caughtB=catB.filter(s=>denyList.test(s)); if(caughtB.length) throw new Error('fixture drift — Category-B was supposed to EVADE the regex (so it stays model-judged), but regex caught: '+JSON.stringify(caughtB)); console.log('C7 OK: deterministic FLOOR holds — regex flagged all',catA.length,'obvious smuggling cases.'); console.log('C7 HONEST CEILING — the following',catB.length,'cases EVADE the regex and REQUIRE MODEL JUDGEMENT (do not claim deterministic):'); catB.forEach((s,i)=>console.log('  [MODEL-JUDGE REQUIRED] '+s)); console.log('correct WHAT-only rewrites: email delivered within 60s | duplicate ids collapse to one record | lookup returns matching record or none');"
checks.desc: Adversarial Canon-TDD smuggling fixtures: asserts the C3 deny-list flags obvious prose HOW ('by calling the SendGrid client', 'iterate the array and push to results', 'use a hashmap to dedupe') AND honestly marks subtler prose as MODEL-JUDGE REQUIRED (regex is a floor, not a ceiling — no overclaim of determinism)
